question: Why do designers opt for compact transmission systems in robotic hand designs?

options.A: To minimize energy consumption by reducing the number of components involved.
options.B: To increase the overall weight of the robotic hand, making it more robust and durable.
options.C: To enhance the aesthetic appeal of the robotic hand, making it visually more appealing.
options.D: To provide single degree-of-freedom grasp forces to each finger, optimizing force types for dexterity.

explanation: Designers select compact transmission systems because they allow the provision of single-degree-of-freedom grasp forces to each finger, which optimizes the types of forces exerted for better dexterity and functionality.
context: Designers instead opt to keep transmission systems compact to improve wearability | provide single degree-of-freedom grasp forces to each finger | provide single degree-of-freedom grasp forces to each finger

hops.context: The SPA pressures are related to their length by a mapping function.
When approximated by a polynomial or exponential, this mapping can be inverted to obtain the length-to-pressure mapping.
When approximated by a polynomial or exponential, this mapping can be inverted to obtain the length-to-pressure mapping.

question: In the context of SPA pressures and their relationship to length, what mathematical expression is typically used to invert the mapping function for obtaining length-to-pressure mapping?

options.A: A linear regression model, generally applied for its simplicity in calculating direct proportional relationships.
options.B: An exponential function, often used due to its ability to model the non-linear relationship effectively.
options.C: A trigonometric function, frequently utilized in periodic or oscillating behavior modeling.
options.D: A logarithmic function, typically employed for translating exponential growth into linear formats.

explanation: The paper specifies that the mapping function relating SPA pressures to their length can be approximated by either a polynomial or an exponential. When approximating with an exponential, it allows for the inversion of this mapping to derive the length from given pressures, indicating the suitability of an exponential function for modeling the non-linear relationships in this context.
context: The SPA pressures are related to their length by a mapping function. | When approximated by a polynomial or exponential, this mapping can be inverted to obtain the length-to-pressure mapping. | When approximated by a polynomial or exponential, this mapping can be inverted to obtain the length-to-pressure mapping.

hops.context: The high power density of the pneumatic systems allows for high force output...
and used it to perform open-loop model-based control.
and used it to perform open-loop model-based control.

question: Which control method is specifically enabled by the high force output of pneumatic systems in soft pneumatic haptic devices?

options.A: Closed-loop feedback control, which continuously adjusts based on sensor feedback.
options.B: Adaptive control, which modifies its parameters in real-time to match changing conditions.
options.C: Open-loop model-based control, which relies on predefined models without feedback.
options.D: Predictive control, which anticipates future states using a detailed mathematical model.

explanation: The question traces the path from the high force output capability of pneumatic systems to the control method used in soft pneumatic haptic devices. The correct answer, A, directly follows the path outlined: pneumatic systems enable high force output, which is utilized in soft pneumatic haptic devices controlled by an open-loop model-based control system, a type of control method that operates without feedback.
context: The high power density of the pneumatic systems allows for high force output... | and used it to perform open-loop model-based control. | and used it to perform open-loop model-based control.
